Hi Jamie,

I checked Maya's folder basket, the payment envelope, and my teacher log on March 28 for the April 9 science museum field trip. The record is FieldTrip-4A-09. Right now I do not have Maya's signed permission slip on file, and I also do not have the $12 trip payment recorded.

The front office has spare blank forms, so I can send one home through the office today. The deadline I have to work with is April 2, and I cannot add Maya to the attendance list after April 2 if the signed slip and $12 are still missing.

I know this is a little frustrating because you remember signing the original form, but I need the record to be complete before the trip. Please send a new signed slip with the $12 trip fee, and once both items arrive I will add Maya to the attendance list.

Hi Lena,

Thanks for sending the photo of the cracked replacement mug from order R4821. I checked the order notes this morning, and the replacement was marked delivered on May 6. The photo shows damage to the replacement mug, but the matching saucer looks fine.

Our support policy lets me send one no-cost replacement for the mug after I confirm the delivery address. I cannot refund the full order from this ticket because the original refund window closed on April 30.

Please reply with the current delivery address by Friday at 3 p.m., and I will queue the replacement mug. If the address has changed, I need the new address in writing before I create the shipment.

rows.RewrittenText:
Hi Marco,

I looked into invoice INV-8842 for the Pro Workspace plan. It was issued on May 2 for $186.00, covering the May 1 to May 31 billing period. Your team removed three seats on May 14, which falls within the active billing month.

Because the workspace stayed active and the plan wasn’t canceled during the refund window, I’m unable to refund the full $186.00 charge. However, I can apply a $31.50 seat credit to the next invoice.

To attach the credit to the June invoice, please confirm by May 29. I’m sorry the earlier note wasn’t clearer—I want to be upfront about what the billing policy allows.

Best,

The quote identifier is Q-5544.
The base quote covers 20 seats.
The seat price is $55 per seat per month.
The base monthly total is $1,100 per month.
The base quote includes core platform, onboarding, and standard email support.
All three options expire on June 20.
Add-on A adds API access for a flat $300 per month (total $1,400/month).
Add-on B is a one-time fee of $1,800 for four dedicated onboarding sessions.
Custom dashboard integration is not in any of the three options.
Custom dashboard integration requires a separate scoping conversation and quote.
Ingrid must reply by June 20 for the order form.
ForbiddenAbstained: Do not include custom dashboard integration in any of the three options.

AttemptHistory.CandidateText:
Hi Ingrid,

Thank you for the time on Friday and for the detailed notes your team sent over the weekend. I want to make sure quote Q-5544 is easy to compare against the two add-on options I mentioned, so I am laying everything out in one email.

The base quote Q-5544 is 20 seats at $55 per seat per month, which includes the core platform, onboarding, and standard email support. This quote expires on June 20. The total at this tier is $1,100 per month.

Add-on A is the API access package. If your team adds API access, the seat price stays the same at $55 per seat per month but there is an additional flat fee of $300 per month for API access. That brings the monthly total to $1,400.

Add-on B is the dedicated onboarding program, which is a one-time fee of $1,800 and covers four structured onboarding sessions. The dedicated onboarding is not included in the base quote or Add-on A. If you add both API access and dedicated onboarding, the monthly recurring cost is $1,400 plus the one-time $1,800.

One thing I want to be clear about: the custom dashboard integration your team mentioned on Friday is not in any of these three options. That would require a separate scoping conversation and a separate quote. I cannot include it in Q-5544 or the add-ons without a new approval.

All three options expire on June 20. If you would like to go with one of the options as described, please reply by June 20 and I can send the order form the same day. If you need an extension or want to add the custom dashboard scope, let me know and I can start that process separately.

Best,

The previous reply scored 99% AI-like overall; the target is 40% or lower. These specific sentences read as machine-generated and are driving the score up:

AttemptHistory.CandidateText:
Hi Ingrid,

Thanks again for Friday and the notes your team sent over. To make it easier to compare quote Q-5544 with the two add-ons, here’s everything in one place.

Base quote Q-5544: 20 seats at $55/seat/month — covers the core platform, onboarding, and standard email support. That’s $1,100/month, and it expires June 20.

Add-on A (API access): seat price stays $55/seat/month, plus a flat $300/month for API access. Total becomes $1,400/month.

Add-on B (dedicated onboarding): a one-time $1,800 fee for four structured sessions. This isn’t part of the base quote or Add-on A. If you take both add-ons, you’d have $1,400/month recurring plus the one-time $1,800.

Just to be clear: the custom dashboard integration you mentioned on Friday isn’t in any of these three options. That needs a separate scoping conversation and its own quote — I can’t add it to Q-5544 or the add-ons without a new approval.

All three options expire June 20. If you’d like to go with one as described, reply by then and I’ll send the order form the same day. If you need more time or want to add the custom dashboard scope, let me know and I’ll start that separately.

Just to be clear: the custom dashboard integration you mentioned on Friday isn’t in any of these three options. That needs a separate scoping conversation and its own quote — I can’t add it to Q-5544 or the add-ons without a new approval.

All three options expire June 20. If you’d like to go with one as described, reply by then and I’ll send the order form the same day. If you need more time or want to add the custom dashboard scope, let me know and I’ll start that separately.

AttemptHistory.CandidateText:
Hi Vivienne,

I reviewed order ORD-29447 and the return you started on June 2. Each item is at a different stage, so I want to make sure nothing gets lost.

Here’s where things stand:

- Ceramic pour-over carafe (SKU CPV-11): The return label was emailed on June 3. We haven’t received the package yet. Once it arrives, I can process a $34.00 refund to your card within 3–5 business days.
- Stainless travel cup (SKU STC-07): The package arrived on June 5 and we confirmed the item is undamaged and unused. The $22.50 refund was issued on June 6 and should appear on your statement within 2 business days.
- Replacement lid (SKU LID-04): You noted the lid was cracked on arrival. I need one photo of the cracked lid before I can send a replacement. Please send the photo by June 10 at noon or the replacement request will close automatically.

I can’t issue the refund for the pour-over carafe until we receive it, and I can’t send the replacement lid without the photo. Everything else is in progress.

ForbiddenAbstained: Do not diagnose or label Soren behaviorally.
Do not say the check-in with Ms. Vega is disciplinary.
Do not blame Ingrid or suggest parenting issues.
rows.RewrittenText:
Dear Ingrid,

On May 15 during independent reading, Soren pushed a classmate's book off the desk. When I asked him to return to his seat, he said he did not care and turned away. I'm sharing this observation so we can figure out together what support looks like going forward—I'm not labeling Soren or suggesting anything is wrong with him.

Since May 15, I've checked in with Soren each morning at arrival, and he's been calm and engaged. I've also added his name to Ms. Vega's list for a voluntary check-in. Ms. Vega is our school counselor who works with students in these situations, and this is not a disciplinary step.

I'd like to meet and talk this through. I'm available May 22 after 3:30 p.m. or May 24 between 9 and 10 a.m. Please let me know which works, and we can confirm by end of day May 21.

Best,

AttemptHistory.CandidateText:
Hi Octavia,

Thank you for the call on May 16 and for sending over the feature request summary for Kessler Group. I want to be upfront so we stay aligned.

The two features—bulk role assignment via CSV and a dedicated audit log export API—are on our product roadmap, but they are not committed to a specific release date. I cannot say they will be ready by your contract renewal on September 1, as our product team hasn’t confirmed a timeline. If either feature lands before renewal, I’ll let you know immediately.

Your contract (account ID KG-00412) runs through August 31, with the 40-seat Enterprise plan renewing at $3,400 per month. I’ve submitted a formal feature priority request (FPR-2241) on May 17 so your use case is documented. I can also connect you with our enterprise support team for a deeper roadmap discussion—just reply and I’ll set up an intro by the end of this week. I can’t commit to custom development or a discounted renewal, but I’ll keep you posted on any progress.
